pulse:
1. The Energy sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 11.1x and a P/E of 17.9x, comparable to broader market multiples of approximately 10.5x and 19.3x, indicating energy valuations are slightly elevated above the market average.

2. Exxon Mobil (XOM) is climbing premarket, trading at an EV/EBITDA of 11.5x and a P/E of 22.8x, both above sector median, as oil prices surge due to geopolitical tensions following recent attacks in the Middle East that disrupt supply (Fox8live.com). The company’s profit margin stands at 8.9%, which is under pressure from rising costs but could benefit from higher oil prices.

3. With escalating tensions in the Middle East pushing oil prices to six-month highs, investors may want to reposition their portfolios toward energy stocks, particularly those like XOM and Chevron (CVX), which have shown resilience despite market volatility. A sustained increase in energy prices could bolster margins and lead to enhanced revenue growth, presenting an attractive entry point for investors focused on long-term energy assets.
